header.headline: Kernel Activations
header.subtitle: Activation kernels should surface the transformation from input state to emitted activation bands.

stage_1.title: Input State
stage_1.body: Kernel Activations starts from normalized or packed inputs that set the compute boundary.

stage_2.title: Kernel Math
stage_2.body: The middle of the diagram should expose the key transformation rather than hide it behind a generic box.

stage_3.title: Fused Path
stage_3.body: Show how kernels fuse, gate, or rotate state before the output contract is formed.

stage_4.title: Output Contract
stage_4.body: The last stage should state what artifact or activation shape the runtime emits.

left.title: Math Trace
left.body: Preserve the local equation or activation story for kernel activations.

right.title: Runtime Trace
right.body: Show the resulting kernel artifact, buffer change, or residual handoff.

footer.note: Kernel Activations belongs in the broader contract because the model needs dense technical layouts, not only charts and posters.
